evals.prompt:
Can you review my resume and give me feedback? I'm applying for backend engineering roles in FinTech companies.

Kemi Adeyemi
kemi.adeyemi@gmail.com | Lagos, Nigeria | github.com/kemiadeyemi

Software Developer

EXPERIENCE

PayBridge Nigeria | Software Developer | Mar 2022 – Present
- Built and maintained backend services for the payment platform
- Integrated with multiple bank APIs for transfers and bill payments
- Worked on KYC verification flow
- Helped fix performance issues in the database
- Participated in on-call rotation

FinAware Tech | Junior Developer | Jan 2021 – Feb 2022
- Developed RESTful APIs using Node.js
- Assisted with database management tasks
- Fixed bugs reported by QA team

EDUCATION
University of Lagos | B.Sc. Computer Science | 2020

SKILLS
JavaScript, Node.js, Express, PostgreSQL, MongoDB, Docker, AWS, Git, REST APIs
evals.expected_output: A structured analysis that: (1) classifies as backend resume, (2) gives a score in the 45-65% range reflecting the weak bullets and missing keywords, (3) identifies at least 5 specific missing keywords like TypeScript, NestJS, Redis, Kafka, CI/CD, (4) rewrites at least 3 bullets with strong verbs + tech + metrics, (5) provides a prioritized top-changes list, (6) notes the missing Summary/headline as a critical gap
expectations: Output clearly classifies this as a backend or backend-leaning resume
An estimated ATS score is provided and falls below 70% (reflecting the weak state of the resume)
At least 5 specific missing keywords are identified by name (e.g., TypeScript, NestJS, Redis, microservices, CI/CD, Kafka)
At least 3 rewritten bullet points are provided that each include a strong action verb, a specific technology, and a measurable outcome or metric
The missing Summary or headline section is flagged as a gap that needs to be added
A prioritized list of top recommended changes is present in the output
The vague 'AWS' listing in Skills is flagged — specific services (Lambda, RDS, SQS, etc.) should be named

evals.prompt:
Here is my resume and a job description I'm targeting. Please analyze how well my resume matches and what I should change.

--- RESUME ---
Michael Torres
michael.torres@outlook.com | +44 7700 900123 | London, UK | linkedin.com/in/michaeltorres | michaeltorres.dev

Frontend Engineer

SUMMARY
Frontend engineer with 4 years of experience building web applications. Skilled in React and JavaScript. Passionate about creating great user experiences.

EXPERIENCE

GrowStack Ltd | Frontend Engineer | Jun 2021 – Present
- Built new features for the SaaS dashboard using React
- Improved loading time of the main page
- Created reusable components for the design system
- Worked closely with UX designers to implement Figma designs
- Added tests for key components

WebAgency Co | Junior Frontend Developer | Sep 2019 – May 2021
- Developed landing pages using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript
- Maintained WordPress sites
- Used jQuery for interactive elements

EDUCATION
University College London | B.Sc. Computer Science | 2019

SKILLS
React, JavaScript, HTML, CSS, Git, Figma, Jest

--- JOB DESCRIPTION ---
Senior Frontend Engineer — FinFlow (London / Hybrid)

We're looking for a Senior Frontend Engineer to join our growing product team. You will own the frontend architecture for our real-time financial dashboard, which serves 200K+ business customers.

Requirements:
- 4+ years of experience in frontend development
- Strong proficiency in React and TypeScript
- Experience with Next.js (SSR/SSG a big plus)
- State management: Redux or Zustand preferred
- Strong understanding of performance optimization (Core Web Vitals, Lighthouse)
- Experience with accessibility (WCAG 2.1 AA)
- Testing: React Testing Library, Cypress or Playwright
- CI/CD experience (GitHub Actions or similar)
- Experience working with design systems
- Nice to have: GraphQL, WebSockets, real-time data, financial domain knowledge
evals.expected_output: A structured analysis that: (1) classifies as frontend resume, (2) runs keyword gap analysis against the JD showing present vs missing, (3) flags TypeScript as a critical gap since JD requires it, (4) flags Next.js, Zustand/Redux, Core Web Vitals, a11y/WCAG, RTL, Cypress/Playwright, CI/CD as missing, (5) notes the vague loading-time bullet and rewrites it, (6) gives a score reflecting partial JD match (probably 50-65%), (7) TypeScript is top priority in changes list
expectations: Output classifies this as a frontend resume
A JD keyword gap analysis is performed — the output distinguishes which JD keywords are present in the resume and which are missing
TypeScript is identified as a critical or high-priority missing keyword given the JD requirement
At least 4 other JD keywords are identified as missing (from: Next.js, Zustand/Redux, Core Web Vitals, a11y/WCAG, React Testing Library, Cypress, Playwright, CI/CD)
The vague bullet 'Improved loading time of the main page' is flagged and a rewrite with a specific metric is provided
Estimated ATS score is provided and reflects the keyword gaps — below 70%
TypeScript appears as the #1 or #2 priority in the top recommended changes list
Existing strengths are acknowledged (4 years experience match, design system work, complete contact info)

evals.prompt:
Hey, I want to improve my resume for a Senior Full Stack Engineer role at a startup — probably Node.js + React stack, cloud-native, somewhere that builds developer tools or B2B SaaS. I have 6 years of experience. Here's my resume:

Samantha Osei
sam.osei@proton.me | Berlin, Germany (Open to remote)
GitHub: github.com/samosei | Portfolio: samosei.dev

Full Stack Software Engineer

SUMMARY
Passionate full stack engineer with 6 years of experience building scalable web applications. I love working with TypeScript, React, and Node.js. I care deeply about code quality and developer experience.

PROFESSIONAL EXPERIENCE

DevTools Corp | Senior Full Stack Engineer | Mar 2022 – Present
- Led development of a CLI tool for local Kubernetes development used by 8,000+ developers worldwide
- Designed the plugin architecture enabling third-party extensions; grew plugin ecosystem from 0 to 45 community plugins in 12 months
- Reduced Docker image build time by 62% through multi-stage build optimization and layer caching improvements
- Built a web-based configuration UI in React + TypeScript that replaced a manual YAML workflow, reducing setup time from 2 hours to 15 minutes
- Implemented end-to-end test suite with Playwright, achieving 87% coverage of critical user flows

CloudPlatform Inc | Full Stack Engineer | Jun 2019 – Feb 2022
- Developed REST API backend using Node.js and PostgreSQL for a B2B analytics platform serving 300+ enterprise clients
- Migrated monolithic architecture to microservices using Docker and Kubernetes, improving deployment frequency from monthly to daily
- Built real-time data pipeline using Kafka and Redis, reducing data latency from 45 minutes to under 30 seconds
- Integrated Stripe API for subscription billing; managed $2M+ in annual transaction volume
- Mentored 3 junior engineers through code reviews and weekly 1:1s

Freelance | Full Stack Developer | Jan 2018 – May 2019
- Built custom e-commerce sites for 6 small businesses using React and Node.js
- Delivered projects on average 15% under budget

EDUCATION
Technische Universität Berlin | B.Sc. Computer Science | 2017

SKILLS
Languages: TypeScript, JavaScript, Python
Frontend: React, Next.js, Tailwind CSS, Redux Toolkit
Backend: Node.js, NestJS, Express
Databases: PostgreSQL, MongoDB, Redis
Cloud & DevOps: AWS (Lambda, RDS, ECS, S3), Docker, Kubernetes, GitHub Actions, Terraform
Testing: Playwright, Jest, React Testing Library
Tools: Git, Jira, Figma, Datadog
evals.expected_output: A structured analysis that: (1) classifies as full-stack, (2) gives a score above 72% reflecting the strong bullets, metrics, and good keyword coverage, (3) identifies a few specific gaps (e.g., GraphQL, observability terms like OpenTelemetry, distributed tracing), (4) gives nuanced Summary feedback, (5) treats this as a strong resume requiring incremental improvement not a total overhaul, (6) provides 2-3 targeted bullet improvements, (7) mentions the thin Freelance section
expectations: Output classifies this as a full-stack resume
Estimated ATS score is above 72% — the resume has good metrics and keyword coverage and should be scored accordingly
The analysis explicitly acknowledges concrete strengths — the specific metrics in bullets, the structured Skills section, or similar positives
At least 2-3 specific keyword or content gaps are still identified (e.g., GraphQL, observability terms, OpenTelemetry, distributed tracing, CDN, or system design language)
The Summary section is reviewed and specific improvement suggestions are given (not just 'looks good')
The top recommended changes list focuses on incremental gains — not a fundamental restructuring of the resume
The Freelance section (two very thin bullets) is mentioned as an area to either strengthen or de-emphasize
